codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested signal/validation logic and extensive unit tests, but two blocking integration defects remain: the new signal extraction and address validation only read snake_case keys while the live outreach enrichment flow passes camelCase leads from public/leads.json. This prevents VAL-OUT-001..004 and VAL-OUT-006 from holding in the real runtime path.

issues.description: build_outreach_prompt() reads enrichment fields only from snake_case keys (fema_declaration_number, permit_status, underpaid_flag, insurance_company, insurer_risk). In production, enrich_outreach.py passes camelCase lead rows loaded from public/leads.json (femaDeclarationNumber, permitStatus, underpaidFlag, insuranceCompany, insurerRisk), so the new FEMA/permit/underpayment/insurer prompt signals are omitted. Evidence: enrich_outreach.py:58 and :108-115 use JSON rows directly; pipeline/leads.py:450-487 serializes these fields as camelCase. Action: support both key styles (or canonicalize before prompting) and add an integration test using camelCase lead fixtures.

validate_outreach_message() checks only lead['address'] for the address-presence rule. Runtime leads from public/leads.json provide propertyAddress, so the check is skipped and address-less messages incorrectly pass. This breaks VAL-OUT-006 in the real enrichment data path. Action: resolve address from both address/propertyAddress (or canonicalize input) and add a test covering camelCase leads.

New tests cover snake_case-only fixtures, which misses the live public/leads.json camelCase shape used by enrich_outreach.py. Add at least one camelCase fixture test for signal inclusion and validate_outreach_message address checks to prevent regressions at the JSON boundary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture docs state snake_case is internal and camelCase is used at the JSON output boundary, but outreach prompt tests and implementation did not enforce compatibility with JSON-shaped lead rows consumed by enrich_outreach.py.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md (camelCase at JSON boundary), enrich_outreach.py:108-115 (reads public/leads.json rows), tests/test_outreach_prompt.py:68-77 (snake_case-only fixtures).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-backend skill requires TDD and full test runs, but it does not explicitly require validating data-shape boundaries for modules used by scripts that consume public/leads.json. This gap likely allowed snake_case-only coverage to pass while integration behavior remained incorrect.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-backend/SKILL.md focuses on RED/GREEN + suite runs; no step calls out verifying camelCase JSON boundary compatibility for enrichment helpers.

summary: FAIL. The commit implements the requested functionality and tests at unit level, but blocking key-shape mismatches prevent the new outreach signals and address validation from working correctly on real public/leads.json data consumed by enrich_outreach.py.
